statement.markdown: Solve the problem and fix the bugs.
statement.inputFormat: Input a number and print if the square root is a perfect square root.
statement.outputFormat: The result should be a boolean value.
statement.notes: The input number will be a perfect square root if its square root is an integer

examples.explanation: Since 4*4=16, the square root of 16 is a perfect square root
The square root of 20 is not an integer

hints.text: Use the math library to check if the square root is an integer

editorial.title: Checking Perfect Square Root
editorial.intuition: Use the math library to check if the square root of a number is an integer
editorial.optimalSolution: Use the math.isqrt() function in Python to check if the square root is an integer
commonMistakes: Not using the math library to check for perfect square root
